question: Under what license is this dataset distributed? Quote the SPDX identifier if any source states it.
keywords: license licensed under released under cc-by cc0 odc-by mit apache gpl creative commons
summary: An SPDX Element containing an SPDX license expression string.
description:
A LicenseExpression enables the representation, in a single string, of a
combination of one or more licenses, together with additions such as license
exceptions.

The syntax for a licenseExpression string is set forth in the corresponding
Annex of this specification
(["SPDX license expressions"](../../../annexes/spdx-license-expressions.md)).
A licenseExpression string is not valid if it does not conform to the grammar
set forth in that Annex.

The ExpandedLicensing profile can be used to represent the complete parsed
license expression as a combination of license objects.

retrieval.hypothetical_passage:
## Licensing Information

The dataset is released under the **Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY-4.0)** license. Users must provide attribution to the original authors, link to the license, and indicate if changes were made. The accompanying code in this repository is dual-licensed under Apache-2.0. Some derivative subsets that include third-party Wikipedia text inherit CC-BY-SA-3.0 and must be redistributed under share-alike terms.

extraction.instruction: Extract the SPDX license identifier under which the dataset is distributed; if multiple licenses apply, return them combined as an SPDX expression.
extraction.field_spec: SPDX property `simplelicensing_LicenseExpression`. Value MUST conform to the SPDX license-expression grammar (e.g. `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`, `CC-BY-4.0`, `CC-BY-SA-3.0 OR MIT`, `GPL-3.0-or-later WITH Classpath-exception-2.0`). Use `NOASSERTION` when sources are silent and `LicenseRef-...` for non-listed licenses.
extraction.output_guidance: Prefer an explicit SPDX identifier from the source over license name prose ('MIT License' -> `MIT`). If the source gives only a free-text license name, map it to the closest SPDX listed identifier; if no match exists, emit `LicenseRef-<slug>`. For dual-licensing use ` OR `; for combined obligations use ` AND `. Return `NOASSERTION` (not 'Not found.') when no license info appears in any source. If dataset and code carry different licenses, return only the dataset license.
